# Promo Optimiser Results View ŌĆö data contract (developer handover)

This describes the JSON the front-end (`Store58_Optimiser_Explainer.html`) consumes, so the
results view can be wired to the real 52-week optimiser output. `dataset.json` in this folder is a
**working sample** (Store 0058, weeks 21ŌĆō23, sales/REVENUE objective) generated by
`compute_dataset.py` from the optimiser workbook ŌĆö use it as the reference payload shape.

The prototype is fully data-driven: it re-aggregates on the fly when the user changes
**time scope** (all / month / week), **metric** (units / sales / AGP), or **benchmark** (plan / last year).
Nothing is hard-coded per item or per week.

## Top-level shape

```json
{ "meta": { ... }, "weeks": [ { ...week... }, ... ], "cuts": [ { ...cut item... }, ... ] }
```

Deliver **all weeks of the plan** in `weeks[]` (52 for a full year). The client groups them into
months by `start` and aggregates whatever subset the user selects. Payload is ~3.5 KB/week, so a
full year is ~180 KB ŌĆö fine to ship in one response, or page by `wk` range.

### `meta`
| field | type | notes |
|---|---|---|
| `store` | string | store id |
| `objective` | string | optimiser objective for THIS run (`REVENUE` / `UNITS` / `AGP`). Show it; the three objective runs reallocate differently. |
| `depts` | string[] | display order of departments |
| `tlab` | string[4] | tactic labels, aligned to the `tac` arrays |
| `dlab` | string[7] | depth-bucket labels for the efficiency chart |
| `hlab` | string[6] | halo break-even bucket labels |

### `weeks[i]`
All metric arrays are ordered **`[units, sales, AGP]`**. All scenarios are `opt` (optimiser),
`plan` (merchant plan), `ly` (last year).

| field | type | meaning |
|---|---|---|
| `wk` | int | promo week number |
| `start` | "YYYY-MM-DD" | week start; used for month grouping |
| `tot` | `{opt:[u,s,a], plan:[u,s,a], ly:[u,s,a]}` | store totals (forecast) |
| `promo` | `{opt:int, plan:int, ly:int}` | count of promoted item-weeks per scenario |
| `tac` | `{opt:[4], plan:[4], ly:[4]}` | item-weeks by tactic, aligned to `meta.tlab` |
| `dmed` | `{opt:%, plan:%, ly:%}` | **median** promo depth, % off. NB: medians don't sum ŌĆö compute per scope server-side if you want exactness; the client currently shows a promo-count-weighted average across weeks. |
| `depts` | `{ DeptName: DeptBlock }` | per-department detail (below) |
| `buckets` | `{off, new, kept, stay}` | store-level promotion-action bridge, each `[╬öunits, ╬ösales, ╬öAGP, item-weeks]`, **vs plan** |
| `dbin` | `[[sumAGP, sumUnits, count] ├Ś 7]` | depth-efficiency: optimiser-promoted items binned by depth (`meta.dlab`). Client shows `sumAGP/count`. Summable across weeks. |
| `hbin` | `[count ├Ś 6]` | halo break-even: switched-off promos binned by required incremental basket margin per unit (`meta.hlab`). Summable. |

**DeptBlock**
```json
{ "opt":[u,s,a], "plan":[u,s,a], "ly":[u,s,a],
  "b": { "off":[╬öu,╬ös,╬öa,n], "new":[...], "kept":[...], "stay":[...] } }
```
`b` is the same four-bucket action split as store `buckets`, but per department (powers the
click-through "what drove it" panel). Buckets are defined **vs plan**; when benchmark = last year
the client hides them.

### `cuts[]` ŌĆö drill-through behind the halo chart
Sample of switched-off promos so the user can audit "basket halo needed to justify the cuts."
Clicking a halo bar lists these, filtered by the bucket (`b`) and the selected weeks (`wk`).

| field | type | meaning |
|---|---|---|
| `wk` | int | promo week |
| `dep` | string | department |
| `n` | string | item name |
| `u` | int | promoted units (planned) |
| `pa` | int | plan AGP (under the planned promo) |
| `oa` | int | optimiser AGP (not promoted) |
| `be` | number | break-even halo per unit = `(oa ŌłÆ pa) / u` |
| `b` | int | halo bucket index, aligned to `meta.hlab` |
| `ps` | string | planned discount value, e.g. "Save 14c" |

The sample embeds the top ~20 cut items per bucket (by units). In production this becomes the
on-demand item endpoint (see below) ŌĆö the halo bar click is exactly the same call scoped to a
bucket. A high `be` = the cut is safe (promo would need implausible halo to beat switching off);
a low `be` = review it. This is the auditability the merchant asked for.

## How each field maps to a panel
- **Summary KPIs** ŌåÉ `tot` + `promo`, delta vs selected benchmark.
- **Department contribution bridge + table** ŌåÉ `depts[*][scenario][metric]`.
- **Department click-through** ŌåÉ `depts[dept].b` (+ on-demand item call, below).
- **"What the optimiser changed" bridge** ŌåÉ `buckets`.
- **Mechanic / depth** ŌåÉ `tac` / `dmed`.
- **Profit by depth** ŌåÉ `dbin`.  **Halo break-even** ŌåÉ `hbin`.  **Downside** ŌåÉ computed from `tot`.

## Bucket definitions (must match optimiser logic)
For each item-week, compare planned store tactic vs optimiser store tactic:
- `off`  = planned promoted ŌåÆ optimiser not promoted
- `new`  = planned not promoted ŌåÆ optimiser promoted
- `kept` = promoted in both
- `stay` = not promoted in either

`dbin` includes optimiser-promoted items only. `hbin` break-even per unit =
`(opt_AGP ŌłÆ plan_AGP) / planned_units` for `off` items with `planned_units > 0`.

## Two things still needed for full sign-off
1. **Real actuals for the accuracy panel.** Today it uses *sample* actuals (deterministic ┬▒%, tagged
   `sample`). Add realised results per week, e.g. extend `tot` with an `act:[u,s,a]` for completed
   weeks, and the client will plot predicted vs actual + true error.
2. **On-demand item detail.** The department click-through references
   `GET /promo/items?store=&dept=&weeks=&metric=` returning the top contributing items
   (name, planŌåÆopt tactic, depth, beforeŌåÆafter metric). Not pre-embedded ŌĆö it would bloat the
   payload at 52 weeks; resolve it lazily on click.

## Regenerating the sample
`python compute_dataset.py` reads the optimiser workbook's `All Weeks` sheet and writes
`dataset.json`. Point it at the production output (same columns) to refresh.
